wpflow.reason: Market demand review concluded MCP-as-product demand is too thin for a business; Zach chose hard delete of working tree. Git history retains commits.
wpflow.lesson: Pressure-test demand on Day 1, not Day 3, for any MCP/developer-tool product.

patterns.mcp_build_recipe: Recon (market/demand/money parallel agents) -> Synthesis (cross-reference overlap) -> Architect + Data-Ingestor parallel -> Builder sequential -> Install + smoke -> GTM
patterns.mcp_auth_gotchas: WordPress Application Passwords skip OAuth review gates; TasteWP is fronted by Cloudflare so any Python client must send a browser UA header (urllib default is blocked with 403 error 1010); httpx default UA works
patterns.mcp_pricing_anchor: 21st.dev Magic at $20/mo hit $10K MRR in 6 weeks — prosumer $9-29/mo is the proven standalone-SaaS MCP band
patterns.backtest_before_ship: Before adding any guardrail/blocking rule, run a SQL backtest on resolved trades for that exact slice. Audit-suggested rules from agents or plans can look right but contradict actual P&L. Example: sub-20¢ entry block looked obvious from 1W-12L record but the 1 win was $95 at 5¢, making the slice net +$0.50 — blocking would have cost $62.
patterns.verify_agent_claims: Audit agents hallucinate confidently. Always Read the cited file/lines before acting on findings. Examples: agent flagged 'no HTTP timeouts in kalshi_client.py' (already had timeout=10 everywhere) and 'split transaction race in get_guardrail_state' (already wrapped in `with get_conn()` context manager).
patterns.dead_markets_not_dead_code: When an integration produces zero results + repeating errors, the underlying market/series may not exist. Don't add retry/backoff to nothing — verify the resource exists first. Kalshi MEM (KXHIGHMEM) was 255 rate-limit errors with zero trades because Kalshi doesn't publish that series.

shipped_fixes: watchdog counter reset on probe exception
